Title: Towards the development of a sustainable soya bean-based feedstock for aquaculture

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates eicosatetraenoic acid (ETA, 20:4 n-3) to eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA, 20:5 n-3).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes EPA accumulation in transgenic soybean seed when stacked with upstream omega-3 pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Δ5 fatty acid desaturase cDNA from Saprolegnia diclina was assembled into a seed-specific cassette and introduced into soybean. 2. When combined with the SDA-producing background and elongase, the four-gene stack accumulated EPA in seed, typically about 1–5% of total fatty acids depending on generation/line. 3. ETA accumulated to higher levels than EPA, indicating incomplete conversion and a downstream bottleneck at or before the Δ5 desaturation step context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in soybean; gene stacking by crossing; Northern blot of immature seed; fatty acid GC profiling of F1, F2, and F3 seeds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; pathway output suggests incomplete conversion to EPA in the stacked system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field production of transgenic soybean lineages
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: fatty acid elongase; Δ6 fatty acid desaturase; Δ15 fatty acid desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Introduced as part of vector pPTN809D5 and later combined with event 535-9 for EPA synthesis. The paper emphasizes that ETA remained the major omega-3 intermediate/product in the four-gene stack.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component of a multigene omega-3 stack for producing EPA-enriched soybean oil for aquafeed; likely needs further pathway optimization with improved upstream flux/channeling to raise EPA toward fish-oil-like levels.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: EPA remained below fish oil levels; substantial ETA accumulation indicates inefficient overall pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Omega-3 LC-PUFA valued as an aquafeed ingredient and for enrichment of harvested fish flesh.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates 18-carbon unsaturated fatty acids including stearidonic acid to corresponding 20-carbon products, enabling ETA formation en route to EPA.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Stearidonic acid (SDA) and other C18 unsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Eicosatetraenoic acid (ETA) and other elongated C20 fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies the immediate EPA precursor ETA and enables EPA biosynthesis in stacked transgenic soybean seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A bifunctional fatty acid elongase cDNA was expressed in soybean under a seed-specific promoter. 2. Expression caused accumulation of elongation products of oleic, linoleic, and linolenic acids, including the omega-3 intermediate ETA. 3. In the four-gene stack, ETA accumulated to high levels and EPA to lower levels, indicating that elongation function occurred but overall pathway flux remained suboptimal.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in soybean; Southern/Northern characterization; GC fatty acid profiling of T1 and stacked progeny seeds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Discussed as inefficient elongation step in overall pathway; remaining SDA suggests elongation bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field production of transgenic soybean lineages
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Δ5 fatty acid desaturase; Δ6 fatty acid desaturase; Δ15 fatty acid desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The source species is not explicitly stated in the provided paper text; the cDNA is described as a bifunctional fatty acid elongase (gift from A.J. Teale). The paper notes inefficient elongation as a likely reason for residual SDA in stacked seed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential component for engineering LC omega-3 soybean oil; improving elongation efficiency is a priority for future engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual SDA and limited EPA accumulation indicate pathway bottleneck; output still below desired fish-oil EPA level.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Omega-3 LC-PUFA valued as an aquafeed ingredient and for enrichment of harvested fish flesh.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates alpha-linolenic acid (ALA) to stearidonic acid (SDA).
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Stearidonic acid (SDA); contributes to EPA in stacked lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers high SDA accumulation in soybean seed and provides substrate for downstream ETA/EPA synthesis in the four-gene and seven-gene stacks.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Event 535-9, carrying a borage Δ6 fatty acid desaturase, was used as the SDA-producing foundation for EPA engineering. 2. Crossing this event with elongase and Δ5 desaturase events enabled seed EPA accumulation. 3. Residual SDA in the final stack indicates incomplete conversion downstream.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior transgenic event incorporated into crosses; fatty acid profiling of stacked progeny seeds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Upstream precursor-generating step; not the main bottleneck emphasized here
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field production of stacked soybean lineages
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: fatty acid elongase; Δ5 fatty acid desaturase; Δ15 fatty acid desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene was not newly introduced in this study but is a core component of the experimental four-gene and seven-gene stacks because event 535-9 was central to EPA production results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key upstream module for omega-3-enriched soybean oil and a foundation for LC-PUFA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual SDA in final oil can rise when downstream conversion is inefficient.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: SDA is an omega-3 feed ingredient and a precursor to EPA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates linoleic acid (LA) to alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), increasing omega-3 precursor supply for SDA and EPA synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Alpha-linolenic acid (ALA); contributes to EPA in stacked lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases omega-3 precursor availability in the SDA-producing event used to build EPA-producing stacks.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Arabidopsis Δ15 fatty acid desaturase was part of event 535-9 used as the parental SDA-producing line. 2. It supports ALA production, thereby feeding the Δ6 desaturase step for SDA accumulation. 3. Its function underpins EPA production in the multigene stack by boosting upstream omega-3 precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of prior transgenic event 535-9 in crosses; fatty acid profiling of stacked progeny seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field production of stacked soybean lineages
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Δ6 fatty acid desaturase; fatty acid elongase; Δ5 fatty acid desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper’s core EPA-production phenotype depends on crossing to event 535-9 containing this gene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply gene in engineering omega-3-enriched soybean oils.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ALA is an omega-3 fatty acid and precursor for SDA/EPA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Committed entry step to carotenoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) to phytoene, initiating carotenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases carotenoid flux in soybean seed, driving β-carotene accumulation alone and supporting astaxanthin production when combined with CrtZ and CrtW.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Soybean events carrying Psy alone accumulated β-carotene to just over 800 μg/g seed. 2. In the three-gene stack with CrtZ and CrtW, Psy supported astaxanthin accumulation up to about 25–38 μg/g seed depending on event/condition. 3. Expression of Psy did not show a clear negative correlation with tocopherol levels, despite the expected competition for GGPP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic soybean events with differential cassette integration; Southern blot; Northern blot; HPLC carotenoid quantification; field validation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Acts as a key flux-pulling entry step into carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse versus field conditions affected carotenoid levels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Event 806-14 carried only Psy and accumulated high β-carotene, demonstrating Psy sufficiency for major precursor build-up. The paper hypothesized competition with tocopherol synthesis for GGPP but did not observe a clear tocopherol decrease.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core entry-step gene for biofortifying soybean seed with provitamin A carotenoids and as a platform for ketocarotenoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lutein decreased (~29% with Psy alone; stronger reduction in full stack); potential precursor competition with vitamin E pathway was hypothesized.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Astaxanthin is a high-value ketocarotenoid used in aquafeeds and nutraceuticals; β-carotene is a carotenoid with provitamin A value.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-carotene hydroxylation branch toward xanthophyll/astaxanthin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates β-carotene and related ketocarotenoid intermediates, contributing to astaxanthin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxylated carotenoids including zeaxanthin-type intermediates en route to astaxanthin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes hydroxylation activity required for conversion of β-carotene-derived intermediates toward astaxanthin in transgenic soybean seed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrtZ was included in the pASTA three-gene stack for astaxanthin production. 2. Event 806-12 carried Psy and CrtZ but not CrtW, enabling comparison of partial pathway activity. 3. Full three-gene expression with Psy and CrtW supported astaxanthin accumulation, while partial stacks accumulated downstream carotenoid intermediates instead.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene integration analysis; Northern blot; carotenoid HPLC profiling across partial and full pathway events
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not identified as the main bottleneck; ketolase step was suggested as less efficient
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field production of transgenic soybean lineages
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes zeaxanthin was not detected and that ketolation appeared inefficient, implying CrtZ was not the dominant limitation in this system.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required component of astaxanthin engineering stacks in seed.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifted carotenoid profile and reduced endogenous lutein in full carotenoid-producing events.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Astaxanthin is a valuable aquafeed pigment and nutraceutical carotenoid.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces keto groups into β-carotene-derived carotenoids, enabling canthaxanthin/astaxanthin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Ketolated carotenoids including echinenone, canthaxanthin, and astaxanthin-related intermediates/products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides ketolation activity for astaxanthin synthesis in soybean seed, but appears to be inefficient and likely limits astaxanthin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrtW was incorporated with Psy and CrtZ in the pASTA stack to engineer astaxanthin synthesis. 2. Events expressing Psy plus CrtW accumulated ketolated intermediates such as echinenone and canthaxanthin. 3. The paper concludes ketolation of β-carotene was inefficient, identifying ketolase activity as a likely limitation for higher astaxanthin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene integration analysis; Northern blot; HPLC carotenoid profiling across partial and full pathway events
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as likely bottleneck/limiting step for astaxanthin production in this system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field production of transgenic soybean lineages
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Minimal accumulation of echinenone relative to β-carotene and the discussion explicitly suggest inefficient ketolation and the need to optimize ketolase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority engineering target for improving astaxanthin yield in soybean seed.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered endogenous carotenoid composition including reduced lutein.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Astaxanthin is a valuable aquafeed pigment and nutraceutical carotenoid.
